instructions.instruction: Call graph_overview or list_pages at session start to orient before doing any work
instructions.original_snippets: Session start: orient by calling `graph_overview` or `list_pages` to surface relevant prior context.
instructions.relevant_when: At the beginning of any session before writing code or analysis

instructions.instruction: After any investigation or analysis, save findings as a page in the vault
instructions.original_snippets: After any investigation or analysis: save findings as a page in the vault.
instructions.relevant_when: After completing code analysis, research, or investigation work

instructions.instruction: Use decision_create for architectural or tooling decisions, recording the question, options, and deadline
instructions.original_snippets: When a non-trivial architectural or tooling choice arises, call `decision_create` on the page where context is richest.
instructions.relevant_when: When a significant technology or architecture choice must be made

instructions.instruction: Use decision_resolve to record the chosen option and rationale when a decision is made
instructions.original_snippets: When a decision is made, call `decision_resolve` with the chosen option and rationale.
instructions.relevant_when: After a decision has been made and the choice is clear

instructions.instruction: Check for an existing page using search or get_page before creating a new one
instructions.original_snippets:
Check for an existing page with `search` or `get_page`.
NEVER create a duplicate page without checking first
instructions.relevant_when: Before calling create_page for any new knowledge page

instructions.instruction: Use namespace/topic naming for all pages (e.g. analysis/auth-flow, plans/migration-v2, investigations/cache-behaviour)
instructions.original_snippets:
Name pages as `namespace/topic` (e.g. `analysis/auth-flow`, `plans/migration-v2`).
NEVER use flat page names without a namespace
instructions.relevant_when: When creating any new page in the knowledge graph

instructions.instruction: Link new pages to related pages with link_pages after creating them
instructions.original_snippets:
Link the new page to related pages with `link_pages`.
Link pages to make knowledge traversable, not isolated.
instructions.relevant_when: After creating a new page that relates to existing pages

instructions.instruction: When the user corrects stored knowledge, call search to find the incorrect block or page first
instructions.original_snippets:
When the user corrects stored knowledge:
1. Call `search` to find the incorrect block or page.
instructions.relevant_when: When the user provides a correction to previously stored information

instructions.instruction: Call update_block or delete_page immediately when correcting knowledge — do not continue until the fix is saved
instructions.original_snippets:
Call `update_block` to fix incorrect content, or `delete_page` if the page is wholly wrong.
Do not continue until the correction is saved — stale memory is worse than no memory.
instructions.relevant_when: After locating an incorrect block or page that needs correction

instructions.instruction: NEVER create a page without first searching for existing content on that topic
instructions.original_snippets:
NEVER create a duplicate page without checking first
BAD: call `create_page("analysis/auth")` without searching for existing auth analysis pages.
GOOD: call `search("auth")` and `get_page("analysis/auth-flow")` before deciding to create.
instructions.relevant_when: Any time create_page is about to be called

instructions.instruction: NEVER save speculative or unverified conclusions — verify findings before writing to the vault
instructions.original_snippets:
NEVER write speculative or unverified content
BAD: save a conclusion drawn from one file without verifying it against other sources.
GOOD: verify findings, then write with a note on confidence level if appropriate.
instructions.relevant_when: Before calling create_page or upsert_blocks with analysis conclusions

instructions.instruction: NEVER skip corrections when the user provides them — update the vault before continuing
instructions.original_snippets:
NEVER skip corrections when the user provides them
BAD: acknowledge a correction verbally and move on without updating the vault.
GOOD: call `update_block` or `delete_page` immediately, then continue.
instructions.relevant_when: When the user says something previously stored was wrong

instructions.instruction: NEVER use flat page names without a namespace prefix
instructions.original_snippets:
NEVER use flat page names without a namespace
BAD: `create_page("migration")`.
GOOD: `create_page("plans/migration-v2")`.
instructions.relevant_when: When naming any new page in the vault

instructions.instruction: NEVER over-document trivial changes — only store findings and decisions that will matter next session
instructions.original_snippets:
NEVER over-document trivial changes
BAD: create a page for every single file edit.
GOOD: create pages for findings, decisions, and patterns that will matter next session.
Trivial one-liner edits with no durable insight.
instructions.relevant_when: When deciding whether a change warrants a knowledge graph entry
